problem: The word "kritisch"/"critical" is used as a summary label for a count that actually mixes Critical- and High-severity findings, without telling the reader the label covers two distinct severities.
evidence: Startkorpus item from issue #511 ("kritisch als Label fuer kritisch + hoch"). Spot-checked against current risk-summary text generation (src/audit/normalized.rs, RiskLevel/legal_flags logic around lines 460-530): risk.summary prose does separate "N critical issues" from "N serious issues" today, but no registry-driven or report-lint check exists yet that would catch a future regression where a single combined count gets labeled just "kritisch"/"critical".
expected: Any UI/report label that aggregates Critical+High severities together must say so explicitly (e.g. "critical or high"), never just "critical"; a lint or registry rule should enforce this rather than relying on manual review of prose strings.
counter_examples: A report that shows Critical and High counts in separate, individually-labeled fields must not be flagged even though both are severity-related counts.
